name: Bopti
description_short: BOPTI Federal Credit Union is a government employee-focused credit union established in 1955, headquartered in San Pedro, California, with $74.6M in assets and 4,100+ members.
description_long:
BOPTI Federal Credit Union was established in 1955 and is based in San Pedro, California. The institution operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution serving primarily government employees in the public sector. With nearly 70 years of operational history, BOPTI has built a foundation of community presence and member loyalty, as evidenced by long-term members like Fred Pearson who has maintained membership for 35+ years.

BOPTI offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans, personal loans, mortgage loans (both fixed-rate options), IRA/KEOGH accounts, and wire transfers. Digital banking services include online loan payments, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and bill pay functionality. The credit union provides financial calculators and comparative interest rate tools to assist members in financial planning.

The institution distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on government and public sector employees, offering tailored financial services for this demographic. BOPTI maintains a single physical branch location at 1451 S Seaside Avenue in San Pedro with dedicated staff (5+ employees as of 2026). The membership base includes diverse account holders: 4,200+ regular savings accounts, 800+ share draft accounts, and 200+ share certificates, reflecting a stable and engaged member community.

However, potential members should note significant limitations: BOPTI operates only one branch location with zero ATMs, severely restricting accessibility and convenience. The main office lobby is currently closed, and there are reports of potential ongoing service issues with online and mobile platforms. For consumers outside the government employee sector or those requiring robust branch/ATM networks, this credit union may not be practical. The institution's small asset base ($74.6M) and limited membership (4,100) indicate it is a niche financial provider rather than a full-service alternative to larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mortgage Loans (fixed-rate 15+ years and 15 years or less)
pros: Established 70-year history (founded 1955) with proven stability and member retention
Specialized focus on government employees with tailored financial services
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, auto loans, CDs, and IRAs
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android platforms
Multiple digital banking services: remote deposit capture, bill pay, e-statements, mobile payments
Competitive auto loan options for new and used vehicles
Strong member satisfaction (5.0-star rating based on available reviews)
cons: Only 1 physical branch location with zero ATMs, severely limiting accessibility and convenience
Main office lobby currently closed, reducing in-person service options
Website reports potential ongoing service issues with online banking and mobile app
Membership restricted to government/public sector employees, excluding general population
Small institution ($74.6M assets, 4,100 members) with limited resources compared to larger credit unions
best_for: Government and public sector employees seeking specialized financial services
Members in the San Pedro, California area with access to the single branch location
Existing long-term members valuing institutional loyalty and personalized service
Consumers comparing mortgage and auto loan rates within a government-focused institution
diagnosis: BOPTI Federal Credit Union is best for government and public sector employees in the San Pedro area seeking specialized financial services with a 70-year-established institution. The primary caveat is severe geographic and accessibility limitations: only one branch, zero ATMs, a currently closed lobby, and potential service issues with digital platforms make this impractical for consumers requiring convenient banking access or those outside the government employee eligibility requirement.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
